normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.16 - Flood and Security Lighting
scope_summary: Controls the amenity impacts of outdoor flood and security lighting on adjoining properties throughout the City of Melville. Sets the conditions under which planning approval is not required (compliance with AS 4282, light contained within the lot, night-time timer adjacent to residential properties) on both private property and City-controlled land, and requires planning approval for floodlight towers on reserves assessed against AS 2560. Does not apply to tennis court lighting, which is governed by LPP 3.4.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: flood lighting on a property adjoining a residential property

cross_references.cited: Local Planning Policy LPP3.4 Tennis Courts
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
AS 4282 - Control of the Obtrusive Effects of Outdoor Lighting

quality_flags: source typo: 'A broad bean of artificial light' in Security Lighting definition (should be 'beam'); appears to be in the original document, not an extraction artifact
policy dated 2016 and predates current terminology ('planning approval' rather than 'development approval')
